source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Above is a cumulative relative frequency plot for the scores of a large university class on a 90-point statistics exam. Which of the following observations is correct?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The distribution of scores is skewed to the right.

choice_map.A: The median score is at least 60 points.
choice_map.B: The distribution of scores is skewed to the right.
choice_map.C: The distribution of scores is skewed to the left.
choice_map.E: If a passing score is 60, most students passed the test.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'Above is a cumulative relative frequency plot', so the plot is central to solving the problem
multi_step: solving requires interpreting a cumulative relative frequency plot rather than reading a single textual fact
the correct option is a distribution-shape claim ('skewed to the right'), which requires mapping visual plot behavior to a statistical concept
there are 5 answer choices, including alternatives about median, symmetry, and passing rate, so the solver must compare multiple candidate interpretations
verifiability: the problem is effectively multiple-choice with 5 explicit options
answer_text exactly matches one listed choice: 'The distribution of scores is skewed to the right.'
answer is short text with clear option-level matching, though registry issues note possible source answer indexing ambiguity

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, one existing image, and an answer are all available.
The image is central to the problem and the required image asset exists, so the core semantics are recoverable.
Verifiability is reasonably strong because the answer is short text that exactly matches one explicit multiple-choice option.
Registry passed and overall scoring is solid, supporting inclusion in the candidate pool.
